confidence_reason: StepSecurity published a same-day technical analysis, GitHub's tags API shows fifteen mutable version tags currently resolving to the same injected commit, and the raw malicious action.yml materially differs from the clean docker-based action definition.

credentials_at_risk: release credentials exposed to GitHub Actions runners

malicious_artifacts: action.yml converted from docker to composite
index.js payload executed through bun run
artifact_analysis.execution_trigger: workflow references a mutable codfish/semantic-release-action tag that now resolves to the injected commit
payload_behavior: executes an obfuscated index.js payload under if: always()
attempts to read Runner.Worker memory for secrets
harvests GitHub OIDC and Personal Access Tokens
attempts propagation into repositories reachable with stolen credentials

lockfile_hunts: Search workflow files and generated action caches for codfish/semantic-release-action refs pinned to the compromised tag set.
filesystem_hunts: Search repositories, Actions cache exports, and retained workspace artifacts for the malicious composite action selectors and commit SHA.
process_hunts: Review runner telemetry for Bun installation followed by bun run against the action checkout path.
network_hunts: Review GitHub API and git push activity from identities exposed to affected workflow runs because the public analysis has not yet decoded a stable external C2 endpoint.
ci_cd_hunts: Enumerate workflow runs that resolved codfish/semantic-release-action mutable tags on or after 2026-06-24T15:39:06Z.

open_questions: The public reporting does not yet identify the initial repository-control mechanism.
The public reporting does not yet decode a stable external exfiltration endpoint.
A maintainer remediation advisory or tag restoration timeline was not available during this refresh.

defender_takeaways.detection: Treat mutable third-party GitHub Action tags as untrusted after 2026-06-24T15:39:06Z unless run records prove a full-SHA pin to a clean commit.
defender_takeaways.hunting: Start by enumerating workflow files, Actions caches, and run metadata for the compromised tag set and the injected Bun/composite-action selectors.
defender_takeaways.remediation: Rotate all credentials reachable from affected workflows, then rebuild or invalidate cached action content on self-hosted runners.
defender_takeaways.prevention: Pin third-party actions to reviewed full commit SHAs and narrow GitHub token and id-token permissions per job.

containment_complete: Mutable codfish/semantic-release-action tags are removed or replaced with reviewed full commit SHAs in all workflows.
Self-hosted runners that executed the affected refs are isolated until cache and persistence review is complete.
eradication_complete: Affected action caches and checked-out workspace artifacts are deleted or rebuilt from clean sources.
No remaining workflow, reusable workflow, or template references the compromised tag set.
recovery_complete: Replacement credentials have been issued from a clean environment and tested in remediated workflows.
Downstream GitHub, cloud, and registry audits show no unauthorized follow-on use.
closure_required: Run-level scoping is complete for all repositories that referenced codfish/semantic-release-action by mutable tag on or after 2026-06-24T15:39:06Z.
Risk owners accept any residual uncertainty caused by missing runner telemetry or missing maintainer disclosure.
